stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM in the current AI landscape.
It is presented via Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its relevance within the open-source ML community.
The tags confirm its function in text-generation using transformer architectures.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core area of text generation within the AI/ML ecosystem.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus totaling approximately 28 trillion pixels, designed for visual generative modeling.
The corpus is highly accessible, featuring 100M training examples and is permissively licensed for both research and commercial use.
GPIC is a curated dataset, having undergone safety filtering and deduplication, addressing key needs for large-scale AI training.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling an optimization push for local AI on Apple devices.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is enabling private, on-device AI functionality for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
The partnership involves ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to enhance open-source safety capabilities.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Introduces LLMSurgeon, a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's pretraining corpus.
Addresses the critical, yet often hidden, issue of LLM 'digital DNA'—the undisclosed composition of training data.
Formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to allow for post-hoc auditing and understanding of model behavior based on data provenance.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable PCB schematics directly from natural language requests.
This addresses a significant bottleneck in electronics design, which is currently manual and expertise-intensive.
The research tackles the challenge of creating LLM-suited representations and acquiring large-scale datasets for PCB design.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in current LLM reasoning by decoupling internal computation from autoregressive token generation.
Proposes a mechanism inspired by human working memory to allow LLMs to manipulate information internally.
Aims to enhance the latent reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models without relying solely on externalized intermediate thoughts.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal measure, the compositional residual (eps*), to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime-computable metric based on system output and coupling constraints to bound compositional incoherence.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon introduces Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to estimate the pretraining data distribution of LLMs.
The research addresses the lack of transparency regarding the 'digital DNA' (training data mixture) of LLMs.
The tool allows auditing of model behavior and failure modes using only generated text from the target LLM.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, a novel LLM designed to generate editable PCB schematics directly from natural language descriptions.
Addresses a significant bottleneck in hardware design: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic creation.
Overcomes key challenges in the field, specifically the lack of LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this task.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) in Large Language Models (LLMs), which is currently slow due to per-query selection and finetuning.
Introduces 'HullFT' to improve the efficiency of TTFT by utilizing Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching.
Aims to resolve the existing trade-off where fast retrieval methods lack quality, while high-quality selection methods are too costly per query.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS),' a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus.
The tool allows researchers to audit or diagnose model behaviors and failure modes using only the LLM's generated text.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first LLM designed to generate editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in electronics manufacturing: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB design.
Overcomes challenges related to the lack of suitable LLM representations and large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal metric, the compositional residual (eps*), to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' behavior.
Provides a runtime-computable method to measure the deviation from a joint coherent polytope based on system output and coupling constraints.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation by providing a clinically realistic, structured dataset (MedCase-Structured).
Focuses on generating HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text, mirroring real-world EHR data formats.
Enables controllable benchmarking of diagnostic reasoning capabilities of LLMs in a clinically relevant setting.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It indicates the use of standard NLP pipelines, specifically 'text-generation'.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, pointing to established ML tooling.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely offline.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within Ollama's ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: The QD-OLED gaming monitor that started it all got a big upgrade
stories.author: Cameron Faulkner
bullet_points: Alienware is set to unveil new gaming monitors at Computex 2026.
The announcement includes two new OLED options for gamers.
A successor to the original 2022 QD-OLED gaming monitor will be debuted with refreshed design and high-end specs.

stories.title: Apple’s strategy for smart glasses is the same as smart watches
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Apple's approach to smart glasses is likened to its strategy for the Apple Watch.
The goal is not just to compete with rivals like Meta, but to disrupt the entire eyewear industry.
The strategy mirrors the Apple Watch's initial market disruption, targeting traditional watch brands like Swatch and Seiko.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure supporting the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
The story falls into the category of cybersecurity threat intelligence and takedowns.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude models) in scientific software development, specifically building a differentiable perturbation theory module (CLAX-PT) in JAX.
It presents a quantified case study detailing 15 supervision events over 12 days, classifying the level of human intervention required.
The findings show the AI agent achieved significant autonomy, resolving ten tasks independently through iteration against oracle tests.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: VideoMLA introduces Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) to video diffusion models.
The core innovation is replacing per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent KV cache.
This addresses memory and latency issues inherent in long-rollout causal video diffusion models.

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies within sequential time-series data.
Highlights the need for natural-language rationales in anomaly detection, as current benchmarks lack this interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to facilitate grounded and interpretable VLM decision-making for time-series analysis.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from base models, can elicit comparable reasoning capabilities to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This sampling method potentially bypasses the need for extensive post-training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary technical hurdle for practical application is developing an efficient sampler capable of accurately 'mixing' to the power distribution.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies, relying on static visual encoders.
The method integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) where fixed aggregation weights cause bias.
Proposes the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to accurately reflect unequal and time-varying client contributions.
Focuses on improving fairness and stability within privacy-sensitive, distributed ML training paradigms.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor research proposals early.
The benchmark is curated from ICLR submissions, providing a real-world dataset for evaluating AI scientific judgment.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to Reinforcement Learning (RL) for improving frontier reasoning models.
It suggests using a 'power distribution' derived from the base model's sampling to achieve comparable reasoning without extra training or verifiers.
A key practical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately 'mix' into this power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) where static reward models fail to capture diverse human preferences.
The core problem is that a single reward model lacks the robustness to generalize to unseen or heterogeneous preference domains.
The proposed method, 'In-Context Reward Adaptation,' aims to allow models to adapt to new human preference distributions without extensive retraining.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM reasoning, which relies on externalizing intermediate thoughts (autoregressive generation).
The paper proposes a method to emulate human cognition by utilizing internal 'working memory' for information manipulation.
This approach aims to decouple internal computation from external communication to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: The research introduces 'COMPOSE' for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The model addresses the limitation of existing methods by considering both the direction of prior work and formal dependencies.
It aims to create 'grounded future mathematical generation' for a given anchor paper.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method proposes achieving strong reasoning capabilities in language models by sampling from a 'power distribution' of the base model.
This approach potentially bypasses the need for expensive post-training techniques like Reinforcement Learning (RL) or curated datasets.
The primary technical hurdle identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately 'mix' into this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical issue in LLM evaluation: many existing pairwise rankings fail to meet conventional resolution targets.
Specific findings show unresolved comparisons in major leaderboards like Open LLM Leaderboard v1 and MMLU-Pro.
The paper proposes framing paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem to improve diagnostic capabilities.

stories.title: 1-Bit Bonsai Image 4B Image Generation for Local Devices
stories.author: modinfo
bullet_points: Introduces a novel image generation technique, '1-Bit Bonsai'.
The key feature is its ability to run image generation locally on consumer devices.
This suggests a trend toward more efficient, decentralized AI model deployment.

stories.title: Unlawful by design: Exposing the human rights costs of generative AI
stories.author: amnesty.org via mccd
bullet_points: The article critiques generative AI, framing its design as potentially unlawful.
It focuses specifically on the human rights implications associated with the technology.
The piece suggests a critical examination of the ethical and legal costs of deploying AI.

stories.title: I Let an AI Agent Hunt Open Source Bounties for 96 Hours — Here's the Brutal Truth About What Actually Works
stories.author: zeroknowledge0x
bullet_points: Explores the practical application of autonomous AI agents in cybersecurity/bug hunting.
Focuses on the real-world performance and limitations of AI in bounty hunting tasks.
Provides an 'honest look' at the effectiveness of delegating complex tasks to AI.

stories.title: What Would a Conscious AI Mean?
stories.author: keithjmackay
bullet_points: The article discusses the philosophical and practical implications of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) achieving consciousness.
It references Anthropic's CEO, indicating high-level industry discussion regarding advanced AI capabilities.
The mention of lawyers drafting documents suggests growing regulatory and ethical concerns surrounding conscious AI.
